sentence: Subjective:

HPI: The patient, Mr. Nelson, presents with ongoing management of rheumatoid arthritis, specifically with complaints of ongoing swelling and pain in his right knee, which started approximately two months ago.
The swelling has progressed to involve the entire leg, and the patient reports pain level of 3/6 on a normal day, but can reach up to 6/6.
The patient attributes the swelling to wearing compression socks, which have been partially effective.
He also reports stiffness when getting up from a seated position, requiring the use of a cane for support.
The patient denies fever.
The patient reports taking Xeljanz 11 milligrams daily and Celebrex 200 milligrams daily.
Past medical history of rheumatoid arthritis.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of rheumatoid arthritis, for which he received a steroid injection in his right knee during a previous visit.
Review of systems: The patient reports no other symptoms in other organs.
Current medications: Xeljanz 11 milligrams daily, Celebrex 200 milligrams daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's knee range of motion is slightly diminished.
Pitting edema is present around the knee and ankle.
Capillary refill is normal in the toes.
The knee is hot, indicating inflammation.
Test Results: Not mentioned.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has ongoing rheumatoid arthritis with worsening inflammation and swelling in the right knee, requiring further management.
Problem list: Rheumatoid Arthritis; Suspected fluid accumulation in the right knee; Swelling of the right leg.
Plan:
I prescribed Xeljanz 11 milligrams daily and Celebrex 200 milligrams daily for ongoing management of rheumatoid arthritis.
I performed a fluid aspiration and steroid injection procedure on the right knee, injecting 80 milligrams of triamcinolone and 1 milliliter of lidocaine.
I will refer the patient for physical therapy to address the swelling and stiffness.
I will also order an x-ray to investigate the cause of the swelling.
I advised the patient to reduce salt intake and to elevate his feet regularly, especially at night.
I will have the patient follow up with his primary care physician for blood work to check his CMP levels.
Follow-up: I instructed the patient to follow up with his primary care physician for blood work to check his CMP levels.
